message:
fix(scheduler): reorder ready-count increment before task injection to prevent race

Fix a race condition in wake_dependents_locked and multiple finalizer
wake paths where inject_ready_uncounted was called before add_ready_count.
A concurrent stealer thread could observe the injected task, steal and
complete it, then decrement the ready count below the value this thread
was about to set — leading to a stale elevated count that never drains.

The fix ensures add_ready_count is called BEFORE pushing any tasks:
- wake_dependents_locked: Batch global tasks into SmallVec<16>, then
  add_ready_count(len), then push all tasks. This also eliminates
  per-task inject_ready_uncounted overhead.
- Three finalizer wake sites: Compute len() from the finalizers vec,
  call add_ready_count first, then iterate to inject.
- drain_ready_tasks: Same reorder pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
